channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ocado Announces 1,000 Job Cuts and Operational Restructuring to Reduce Costs
item.description: Ocado, the UK-based online grocery and automated distribution technology company, has announced plans to cut around 1,000 jobs as part of a broader restructuring effort aimed at reducing costs. Two-th...

item.title: Major UK News Outlets Form Coalition to Set AI Content Usage Standards
item.description: Sky News has joined forces with the Financial Times, The Guardian, The Daily Telegraph, and the BBC to create a new coalition called the Standards for Publisher Usage Rights (SPUR). The coalition aims...

item.title: Asian Businesses Face Uncertainty After US Supreme Court Blocks Trump's Tariffs
item.description: The US Supreme Court recently struck down the legal basis for former President Donald Trump's tariff regime, leaving Asian exporters, manufacturers, and logistics companies in a state of uncertainty. ...

item.title: 'Scrubs' Revival Explores J.D. and Elliot’s Divorce, Turk’s Stagnation, and Dr. Cox’s Retirement
item.description: Sixteen years after the original run of 'Scrubs,' the beloved medical comedy returns with a two-episode premiere, treating fans to a mix of nostalgia and new dynamics. The revival assumes the series c...

item.title: Study identifies the most commonly misspelt words among UK schoolchildren
item.description: A large-scale study of nearly a million pupils in the UK has identified the words most frequently misspelt by schoolchildren. Analyzing 530 million spelling attempts, researchers found that mistakes a...

item.title: Andrea Pavan Recovering After Lift Accident Ahead of South African Open
item.description: Italian professional golfer Andrea Pavan is currently recovering in hospital after suffering multiple injuries in a fall down a lift shaft at his private accommodation near Stellenbosch Golf Club. The...

item.title: Greens win Gorton and Denton by-election as Labour falls to third place behind Reform
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer is facing renewed pressure after Labour suffered a heavy defeat in the Gorton and Denton by-election, losing one of its safest seats to the Green Party and slipping into third place b...

item.title: Court Backlogs Leave Victims Waiting Years for Criminal Trials Amid Reform Dispute
item.description: Victims of crime in the United Kingdom are facing severe and prolonged delays in seeing cases brought to trial, as the backlog in the Crown Court system continues to grow. Dozens of serious criminal c...

item.title: Expert review of the top baby monitors for 2025, featuring audio, video, and smart options
item.description: This Telegraph Recommended guide, written by a parent who has tested numerous models, explores the evolution of baby monitors from simple audio devices to advanced Wi-Fi-enabled video systems with fea...

item.title: Scientists Explain the Mechanism Behind Antarctica’s Blood Falls
item.description: The Blood Falls, a striking natural phenomenon in Antarctica's McMurdo Dry Valleys, have fascinated scientists for over a century. First discovered in 1911 by geologist Thomas Griffith Taylor, these c...

item.title: UK Launches First Deep Geothermal Power Plant in Cornwall
item.description: The UK is set to inaugurate its first deep geothermal power plant in Cornwall, providing renewable electricity to around 10,000 homes. Developed by Geothermal Engineering Ltd (GEL) over nearly two dec...

item.title: Violence Erupts in Puerto Vallarta After Reported Killing of Cartel Leader
item.description: Violence broke out in Puerto Vallarta, Mexico, following the reported killing of cartel leader Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, known as “El Mencho,” by Mexican military forces. In apparent retaliation, su...

item.title: Oxford Street to be pedestrianised following official approval
item.description: The Mayor of London, Sir Sadiq Khan, has granted final approval for the pedestrianisation of Oxford Street, instructing Transport for London (TfL) to remove traffic from the stretch between Great Port...

item.title: Impact of 2026 F1 Rules on Driver Skill and Performance
item.description: The introduction of Formula 1's 2026 regulations has prompted debate on whether driver skill remains a decisive factor in racing performance. Top drivers like Max Verstappen, Lewis Hamilton, Fernando ...

item.title: Trump Criticizes Democratic Lawmakers and Robert De Niro Following State of the Union Address
item.description: President Donald Trump sharply criticized Democratic Representatives Ilhan Omar of Minnesota and Rashida Tlaib of Michigan, as well as actor Robert De Niro, following a tense State of the Union addres...

item.title: Hannah Spencer becomes Green Party MP after Gorton and Denton by-election win
item.description: Hannah Spencer, a 34-year-old plumber from Greater Manchester, has become the Green Party's first ever Westminster by-election winner, securing the Gorton and Denton seat. Spencer began her working li...

item.title: Green Party by-election victory in Gorton and Denton raises strategic questions for Labour
item.description: The Green Party has secured a historic victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election, marking the first time it has won a Westminster by-election. The result represents a significant political moment a...

item.title: Recipe for a Versatile Buttery Cookie Dough
item.description: The article was intended to provide readers with a straightforward recipe for a versatile, buttery cookie dough suitable for a variety of baking purposes. Unfortunately, access to the content has been...

item.title: Industry Season Four Expands Ambitiously but Struggles to Maintain Its Core Appeal
item.description: The fourth season of the HBO/BBC drama Industry has reached ambitious heights, yet this expansion has come with notable missteps. Once a tightly focused series on junior employees navigating the high-...

item.title: Fresh forensic analysis challenges key silencer evidence in Jeremy Bamber conviction
item.description: A new forensic review of crime scene photographs has raised further questions about the safety of Jeremy Bamber’s 1986 conviction for the murders of five members of his family at White House Farm in E...
